title: Measure lengths (inch, ft)
description: Practice measuring items on a ruler. All items are measured in inches or feet.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the width of the tennis ball?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d568555b3bacb2399e99c86e4980f995e2d7978e)


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] inches
hints:
The width starts at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  

Where does the width end?
The width ends at $2\text{ in}$.
The tennis ball's width is $2$ inches.

hints:
The length starts at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  

Where does the length end?
The length ends at $4\text{ in}$.
The mouse's length is $4$ inches.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the length of the shark?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3e804cdb438692962e568876fc8b3343a0afdead)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] feet
hints:
The length starts at $0\text{ ft}$ on the ruler.  

Where does the length end?
The length ends at $7\text{ ft}$.
The shark's length is $7$ feet.
First, let's locate $8\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

Here is $8\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bcf5e8da8728742ccd1b2e161aa13a106cba286a)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1801f92527fd446406df26e5943454fe7c8fc80d)

Which pencil ends at $8\text{ inches}$?
Pencil $\redE{A}$ measures $8\text{ inches}$.
First, let's locate $3\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

Here is $3\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/477cb4d69693a918d4371ebd3f1c9e87ce28ce8b)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f51206fd3d09d5903ce1e08ee28d9c1a3bead106)

Which pencil ends at $3\text{ inches}$?
Pencil $\blueE{B}$ measures $3\text{ inches}$.
First, let's locate $5\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

Here is $5\text{ inches}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2cce39933219c1e26fcfb9793328ff2d84ecfb8d)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7f5a9c133981ffc28e53c714a9a5a4b7103ce546)

Which pencil ends at $5\text{ inches}$?
Pencil $\greenE{C}$ measures $5\text{ inches}$.
First, let's locate $1\text{ inch}$ on the ruler.

Here is $1\text{ inch}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b2b03944a2136b5ef11938351296b1a3d31d5dd6)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ in}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aa056b98c45c40103403ae52b818eb32d744fc3)

Which pencil ends at $1\text{ inch}$?
Pencil $\purpleD{D}$ measures $1\text{ inch}$.
